title: Wall-Mounted Boot Rack
summary: A single 2x8 board drilled for a row of dowel pegs — boots slide over the dowels to store upright and off the floor, drying and staying paired.
description:
This is about as simple as wall storage gets: one 2x8 board, drilled with a row of holes, fitted with short dowel pegs that boots slide down over. The peg spacing is the one number worth getting right — 4-1/4" center to center with 2-1/2" clearance from each end, close enough for a full row of boots without them crowding each other.

Drill the dowel holes about 3/4" deep with a Forstner bit sized to match the dowel diameter — a Forstner bit gives a flat-bottomed hole, which matters here since the dowel needs to seat squarely rather than wobble in a rounded drill-bit hole. **Drill a smaller pilot hole inside each larger hole, all the way through the board's back**, before gluing any dowel in place — that pilot hole is how a screw reaches through the board and into the dowel from behind, since the whole rack hangs on the strength of the dowel-to-board joint, not just glue.

Glue every dowel into its hole, then mount the board to the wall. **This must go into real studs** — a rack loaded with wet winter boots is real weight pulling straight out from the wall, and drywall anchors alone are not rated for that kind of repeated pull-out load.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Mark the dowel holes
steps.body: Mark the 2x8 board for eleven dowel holes, spaced 4-1/4" apart center to center, with 2-1/2" clearance from each end.

steps.title: Drill the dowel holes
steps.body: Drill each dowel hole about 3/4" deep with a 1-1/4" Forstner bit sized to the dowel diameter.

steps.title: Drill the pilot holes
steps.body: Drill a smaller pilot hole inside each larger hole, all the way through the back of the board, for a screw to reach through into the dowel from behind.

steps.title: Glue and secure the dowels
steps.body: Glue each dowel peg into its hole, then drive a screw through the back pilot hole into each dowel for a secure, load-bearing joint. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Mount to the wall
steps.body: Locate wall studs and mount the rack directly into the studs — drywall anchors alone are not rated for the repeated pull-out load of loaded boots.

unresolvedImages.alt: Wall-mounted boot rack with dowel pegs
